description: A social scientist at a university's Center for Comparative Policy Studies needs their Chrome browser configured according to the Research Workstation Specification. Organize 30 international data-source bookmarks into 5 specific folders, configure multilingual browsing support and spell check, enable experimental flags for research workflow optimization, set up custom search engine shortcuts, and apply privacy and download settings for accessing sensitive government data portals.
instruction: The complete 'Center for Comparative Policy Studies — Research Workstation Configuration Standard v4.0' is located on your Desktop at ~/Desktop/research_workstation_spec.txt. Read it carefully and implement ALL requirements exactly. You must organize the flat bookmarks into the specified hierarchy, enable the required chrome://flags, add the requested languages in chrome://settings/languages, configure the search engine shortcuts (oecd, scholar, wb), and set the specified homepage, startup, privacy, and download preferences.
